Meta Open-Sources Its 8-Year Internal Design System: AI-Friendly, 150+ Components, Powering 13,000+ Internal Apps, a Potential Alternative to Material UI, Ant Design, and Chakra UI. Tailwind Killer?

5 min read
2 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

meta astryxMeta (formerly Facebook) has open-sourced Astryx, a design system they've been using internally for 8 years. The name might sound unfamiliar, but it was once Meta's largest and most core internal design system, powering tens of thousands of applications.
Project Overview
If you've worked in frontend, you're definitely familiar with names like Material UI, Ant Design, and Chakra UI. But did you know that Meta has been using a design system internally that was never publicly available?
It boldly describes itself as a design system "built for the era of humans and AI agents coexisting." That positioning might sound ambitious, but after exploring its design philosophy, you'll notice it incorporates forward-thinking details in many aspects.
This is an open-source design system that has evolved over eight years within Meta, becoming the most widely used and largest design system in the company—powering over 13,000 applications, shaped daily by the engineers, designers, and product teams who rely on it.
